title: RAG Architecture Review
summary: Pressure-test retrieval, chunking, citation, and eval decisions before a knowledge feature ships wider.
whenToUseIt: Use this before launching or materially changing a retrieval-backed AI workflow.
prerequisites: A working prototype or architecture draft
Access to sample queries and source documents

steps.title: Define the product promise
steps.description: State what the retrieval flow must do, what it must not do, and what evidence the user should see in the UI.
steps.tips: Write the promise in user language first, then translate it into system constraints.

steps.title: Inspect ingestion and chunking
steps.description: Review how documents enter the system, how they are split, what metadata survives, and where stale content can leak through.

steps.title: Check retrieval observability
steps.description: Confirm you can see the retrieved chunks, similarity scores, citations, and failure cases for each representative query.
steps.aiPrompt: Review this retrieval design and identify the most likely trust failures if citations or chunk selection drift.

steps.title: Run eval cases
steps.description: Use representative prompts to test correctness, groundedness, stale-content risk, and abstention behavior.

steps.title: Decide release conditions
steps.description: Document the quality threshold, rollback trigger, and the next instrumentation gap to close before launch.

expectedOutcome: A retrieval-backed feature with a visible quality bar, known failure modes, and clear release gates.
commonMistakes: Treating prompt quality as a substitute for retrieval quality
Launching without citations or retrieved-chunk visibility
Using stale or unversioned knowledge sources
